tokens: On page 1-2 where you get to setting your S-Band to Slew, I've got some new angles for you because of your attitude.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Copied...(Stops to listen to the correction) Okay; a plus 71 and a minus 58.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We'll give you a little quick summary here before we get on with it.
The general terrain looks exactly like what you had on 14.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And many of the craters that we use for ID were completely washed out with no shadows, and that's probably because the topo data just wasn't that good.
And I think we're sitting a little off in attitude, but we're in fairly good shape.
And, when we get around to the SEVA (Stand-up EVA), we'll try and pin down the location exactly.
I had a little bit of dust at 150 (feet) and completely obscured at 50 feet.
It was IFR (Instrument Flight Rules) from then on down.
And the rest of it, you could probably see it (from the telemetry) as well as we could.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Do you have an estimate of your landing site?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: There's a long pause there, Ed.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think...As best I could find, I think we're fairly close to Salyut.
But I guess the best thing to do is to press on and get to the SEVA where we can take a look around.
It's very hummocky, and, as you know, in this kind of terrain, you can hardly see over your eyebrows.
There's very little to tell us exactly where we are in our local position.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You can probably explain it to the folks back there better than I could.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dick (Gordon, the backup Commander) wants to know where Falcon Crater is, if you can see it?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Over background laughter in Houston) That's what we figured.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I have your camera Pads for you.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Understand mapping camera and pan camera Pads are the same.
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: Your Rev 16 map camera Pad...(Pause while he gets an instruction from the Flight Director) Endeavour, Houston, give us
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: You've got Accept, and I'm in a middle of a maneuver (that is, a spacecraft re-orientation) right now.
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: If you'll give us an Enter, we can go ahead with the load.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Could I talk with (you) a minute?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Go ahead, Houston, Hadley Base here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: The President sends his regards through (NASA Administrator) Dr.
(James) Fletcher, and I read: "The President sends his congratulations to the entire ground team and the Apollo 15 crew on a successful landing and sends his best wishes for the rest of the mission."
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: ...to the President, and I'd like to thank you, too, for the support we've had.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Houston, the Hadley Base here.
Tell those geologists in the Backroom to get ready because we've got something for them.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Which group of guys in the back room?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: If you've got the gyro torquing angles, I'll torque them out on the minute.
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: I'll wait until the even minute to torque them out.
transcript.speaker: Worden

tokens: Stand by on your stars, and let us give you some new ones.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: The first pair, we will have
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Second pair, we'll have star 61 - that's Epsilon Orionis - in detent 6.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And you look good from the ground, Al.
We'll see you on the other side.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Standing by on the RLS (Reference Landing Site).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: When the Noun 88 (the star coordinates) comes up again, hold it, please.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I didn't copy the name of the star, but how about Castor or Pollux or Betelgeuse or somebody like that?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We'll tell you which one it is; we think it's in the middle of the belt (in the constellation of Orion), Dave.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We can use this one, but we don't have the
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That's what we gave you, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Houston, why don't you read us the Noun 88s again then, please?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; that's exactly what we just loaded.
We'll see where it takes us.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We're seeing it...On the last register, we're seeing a minus 02124.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And...Let's take a look at the
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Dave, we specified it for detent 6, and the computer's giving you detent 5.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I should have noticed that, Ed; I'm sorry.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Dave, the spiral should be about 330 and the cursor about 148, if that helps.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: If you'd like some help being coached on to this next star, it's in (the constellation) Cassiopeia, and we're suggesting detent 3.
(It should) be a spiral of 181, a cursor of 23, and it should be just to the left of Navi, the bright star to the left of Navi.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We'll to have to rerun that first star again.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: The reason I did that was because we need to stick an erasable load in here on the
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It doesn't look like the program is running exactly right.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: What seems to be the problem, Dave?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Sounding a bit doubtful) Okay, we'll try; but we're in a loop here where it won't accept detent 6, I believe; but we'll press on.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It's always going to give the first one it computes, Dave.
(Pause) Change it to (detent) 6 and go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, Dave, you ready for your second
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Well, I think we've got them on board if that's what you read us.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Houston; the torquing angles are up.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) And how about the RLS (Reference Landing Site)?
Do you want to take it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We need to redesignate the rendezvous radar to 180, 270, 00 for thermal protection.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: As soon as you get the radar parked, we're ready for the E-Mod dump.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, Houston; here comes your E-Mod dump.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Houston, (as per checklist page Surface 1-6) we'll be standing by for your Stay NoStay.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Roger, you have a Stay.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We're going to delete the 10-minute delay, so you can go right into P06 and power down.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We're having changeover (of flight control teams) down here; didn't get a chance to say "real good job" on that descent.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And (we) appreciate all your help, too.
The comm was super today, and everybody back there was right on top of it all the way.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And looked real good from here, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Hello there, Houston; how are you?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Super down here, Dave and Jim.
From what I heard, it was not only a good landing, it was a great landing.
And it sounds like you didn't even bend anything.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: But we're sure in a fine place here.
George (Crater); it looks like it's right over a little rise.
I'm sure it's much farther than that.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, we've got some vital questions down here.
First, did you see the rille on the way down?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And did you read the VHF call from Endeavour right shortly before the landing?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Did he have something to say?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'm sure he did, Dave; but we're wondering if you ever heard him call you on VHF.
It sounds like we're going to have to do a VHF comm check.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: No, we tried that comm check 3 minutes prior to PDI and we got no response.
And Houston verified that the Endeavour could hear us, but we did not hear him.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And we have Rev 16 through 20 lift-off times when you're ready.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay; give us 5 minutes to clean up the cockpit here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) And be advised the Backroom's doing slow rolls just from your first description there.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, we've got a lot more coming.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'm standing by to copy some lift-off data.
transcript.speaker: Irwin
